title: abrus madagascariensis var. typicus
intro: Abrus madagascariensis var. typicus is a plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family, commonly known as the coral bean or rosary pea. It is native to Madagascar and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant's leaves, bark, and seeds are used in herbal preparations to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, and to promote wound healing and digestive health. Herbal preparations of Abrus madagascariensis var. typicus include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal benefits.
